mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive quarterly returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of periods following industrial production acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in > 60% of months following inflation spikes above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions show no statistically significant predictive power for 20-day equity returns with a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces near-term earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX advances in > 50% of periods following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
